rulings.Case Title: UNIFUND CCR PARTNERS, A PARTNERSHIP DOING BUSINESS VS. ANNETTE D BONIFACIO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Why Defendant/Judgment Debtor'S Interest In Real Property Should Not Be Sold To Satisfy Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2020, Line 1, Order To Show Cause Why Defendant/Judgment Debtor's Interest In Real Property Should Not Be Sold To Satisfy Judgment. Hearing required. It appears from the proof of service filed on February 7, 2020, that the judgment creditor has not complied with Code of Civil Procedure section 704.770(d), which provides that no later than 30 days before the time set for hearing, the judgment creditor shall both serve the judgment debtor personally or by mail a copy of the order to show cause, the application of the judgment creditor, and the notice of the hearing; and shall personally serve a copy of those documents on an occupant of the dwelling or, if there no acceptant present at the time service is attempted, post a copy of each document in a conspicuous place at the dwelling. The proof of service shows that the OSC was posted in a conspicuous place, but not that it was served personally or by mail on the judgment debtor. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. JOHN DOE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday February 13, 2020. Line 2 - DEFENDANT JOHN DOE's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: As plaintiff does not dispute that the responses were not timely, all objections have been waived. Plaintiff is ordered to provide responses to request for production demands 1-24, form interrogatories 2.2, 2.5, 2.6, 2.7, 4.1(c), 6.2, 6.3, 6.4, 8.3, 8.4, 8.5, 14.1, and special interrogatory 25. Plaintiff is ordered to pay defendant sanction in the sum of $2,560. Further responses and sanctions are to be paid on or before March 15, 2020. The court has the power under Code of Civil Procedure sections 2030.090 and 2031.060, to make further orders to protect the identity of the parties. Pursuant to this power, the court will limit at this time the disclosure of any identifying information solely to defense counsel and defendant to allow plaintiff's new counsel to make a motion regarding the future handling of material regarding the identity of the parties within the next 30 days. If such a motion is made, the identity information shall not be disclosed by defense counsel or defendant until the motion is decided. If no motion is filed within 30 days, the restrictions on dissemination of the information shall be removed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CORNERSTONE LAW GROUP VS. SUSAN BIBA
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2020, Line 5, PLAINTIFF CORNERSTONE LAW GROUP MOTION For Preliminary Injunction. Plaintiff Cornerstone Law Firm's motion for preliminary injunction is denied. "In determining whether to issue a preliminary injunction, the trial court considers two related factors: (1) the likelihood that the plaintiff will prevail on the merits of its case at trial, and (2) the interim harm that the plaintiff is likely to sustain if the injunction is denied as compared to the harm that the defendant is likely to suffer if the court grants a preliminary injunction. [Citation.] 'The latter factor involves consideration of such things as the inadequacy of other remedies, the degree of irreparable harm, and the necessity of preserving the status quo.'" (Donahue Schriber Realty Group, Inc. v. Nu Creation Outreach (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 1171, 1177.) [The Court's complete tentative ruling was emailed to the parties.] Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
